content.full_text:
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 27, 1999

Armando Gomez
Elections Clerk
Ethics Commission
City and County of San Francisco
1390 Market Street, Suite 801
San Francisco, California 94102-5302

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-99-259

Dear Mr. Gomez:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Because your questions are general in nature, we are treating your letter as a request for informal assistance.²

QUESTIONS

1. May a committee file a termination statement retroactive to the date the committee ceased activity and avoid filing subsequent semi-annual statements?

2. May a committee combine reporting periods for long periods of inactivity and avoid filing several semi-annual statements?

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

Our File No. 1-99-259
Page 2

CONCLUSIONS

1. Yes, a committee may file a retroactive termination statement (Form 410 - Statement
of Organization Recipient Committee) provided the termination criteria set out below has been
met.

2. Yes, a committee may file one campaign statement (Form 460 - Recipient Committee
Campaign Statement) for each preceding calendar year in which statements were not filed. For
the current calendar year, a statement must be filed for each applicable reporting period.

ANALYSIS

A committee may file a retroactive termination statement (Form 410) to the date in which
the candidate or committee:
(1) Had ceased to receive contributions and make expenditures and did not anticipate
receiving contributions or making expenditures in the future;
(2) Had eliminated or declared that it had no intention or ability to discharge all of its
debts, loans received or other obligations;
(3) Had no surplus funds; and
(4) Had filed all required campaign statements disclosing all reportable transactions.
(Regulation 18404.)

It is also permissible for a committee to file one campaign statement (Form 460) for each
preceding calendar year in which statements were required, but never filed. For the current
calendar year, however, a statement must be filed for each applicable reporting period (i.e.,
January 1st through June 30th and semi-annually thereafter until the committee terminates). It is
important to note, that as a filing officer, you have the ability to impose (or waive) fines based on
each statement that was required. In other words, if a committee files one statement covering
the entire year of 1997, you may still impose fines based on the due dates for the statements that
should have been filed (e.g., two semi-annual statements).

If you have any questions regarding this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant
